extraction.one_line_summary: GIRMAY Biniam became the first Eritrean Grand Tour stage winner by outsprinting VONDERPOOL in Jesi after final-kilometer GC probing attacks neutralized
tactical_patterns: POZZOVIVO led out GIRMAY from deep, suggesting Intermarché committed domestique firepower specifically for reduced-bunch sprint finishes.
CARAPAZ and CARTHY attacked inside 1km despite no GC time gaps available, revealing nerves about positioning loss.
Pure sprinters YUEN and CAVENDISH dropped 76km and 60km out respectively on minor gradient before classified climb began.

rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Won from reduced bunch after nearly taking wrong turn 6km out, showing composure recovery under pressure
rider_signals.VONDERPOOL Mathieu: neutral: Attacked final 6km but refused cooperation from YATES/FORMOLO/CICCONE, suggesting unfamiliarity with breakaway negotiation tactics
rider_signals.YUEN Caleb: negative: First sprinter dropped 76km from finish before main classified climb, exposing weakness on extended false-flat approaches
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Mechanical at 60km required chase despite easier gradient than subsequent climbs, fitness/positioning vulnerability signal
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: positive: Deployed as leadout man instead of GC support, indicating Intermarché tactical flexibility for opportunistic stage hunting
